title: Disgraceful! Pro-Life Establishment Lies About Abolitionist Candidate Warren Hamilton
author: James Silberman
orientation: James Silberman defends Oklahoma State Senate candidate Warren Hamilton against a dark-money mailer that accused him of being weak on abortion, arguing the attack came from a pro-life establishment that uses incremental compromise bills to perpetuate abortion's legality. The piece dissects three specific bills (SB1721, SB1728, HB1396), invokes 19th-century abolitionist arguments against gradualism, and frames the race as a fight between regulating abortion (Boggs) and abolishing it (Hamilton).
audiences: Oklahoma voter weighing the Boggs vs. Hamilton primary
Legislator or staffer evaluating incremental bills
Historian comparing antislavery and antiabortion rhetoric
Abolitionist activist seeking talking points against compromise

questions.question: What is Warren Hamilton actually running on, and how does Silberman distinguish that from typical pro-life candidacies?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter weighing the Boggs vs. Hamilton primary
questions.answer: Hamilton is running on total, immediate abolition rather than negotiation, delay, or symbolic regulatory victories. Silberman frames every other posture, including the kind that the establishment rewards on surveys, as a way of avoiding abolition.
questions.quote: Warren Hamilton is running for Oklahoma 7th Senate District on a platform to completely abolish abortion. He is not running to compromise with it. He is not running to delay abolition with more regulations that do nothing to establish justice.

questions.question: Why did Hamilton refuse to fill out the Oklahomans for Life survey, and what does Silberman say a perfect score on it actually requires?
questions.answer: Silberman says OFL has been the chief obstacle to the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act since 2016, and that earning a 100 percent score requires opposing abolition and supporting bills that allow babies conceived in rape to be killed. Hamilton declined the survey on those grounds.
questions.quote: To get 100% on OFL's survey, as Boggs did, [a candidate has to agree to oppose the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act and support legislation that allows for human beings conceived in rape to be murdered]. Warren did not fill out OFL's immoral survey because he is running to abolish abortion.

questions.question: How does Silberman explain the political function that incremental pro-life bills served for Senator Greg Treat in 2019?
questions.audience: Legislator or staffer evaluating incremental bills
questions.answer: After Treat killed SB13 and faced public outrage, he authored a trigger bill and helped pass three incremental bills that did nothing to abolish abortion but rehabilitated his pro-life reputation among voters who didn't grasp the maneuver.
questions.quote: What these bills did accomplish, though, was help Treat save face. The outrage against him was somewhat quelled. These bills preserved Treat's pro-life reputation in the minds of those who did not understand what he was doing.

questions.question: Beyond political cover, what cultural damage does the article say incremental bills inflict?
questions.answer: Silberman argues these bills teach the public dehumanizing falsehoods, citing a woman who justified her abortion because the baby couldn't feel pain (a line she absorbed from the Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act) and an Ohio senator who believed life begins at a heartbeat because of the Heartbeat Bill.
questions.quote: Furthermore, incremental compromise bills teach people dehumanizing misinformation about preborn human beings. Free the States lobbyist Russell Hunter discovered this when he was outside a killing center in Norman trying to talk to abortion-minded mothers and fathers in 2016. After engaging one young woman who was going in for an abortion, she told Russell that she was pro-life, but that the abortion was okay because the baby was too young to feel pain.

questions.question: What does Silberman say is really blocking abolition in Oklahoma, given Republican supermajorities?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter weighing the Boggs vs. Hamilton primary
questions.answer: He insists the obstacle isn't a vote count but an ideology: Republicans choose to pass regulations instead of an abolition bill they actually have the votes to pass.
questions.quote: In a state like Oklahoma where pro-life Republicans hold super-majorities in the Senate and the House, it's not that we cannot pass a bill abolishing abortion because we do not have the votes, **it's that we could pass a bill abolishing abortion but choose to pass pro-life bills regulating abortion instead.**

questions.question: How does the article use 19th-century antislavery voices to indict gradualism?
questions.audience: Historian comparing antislavery and antiabortion rhetoric
questions.answer: Silberman quotes the American Anti-Slavery Society's 1833 Declaration of Sentiments, William Lloyd Garrison's 'NO COMPROMISE WITH SLAVERY' banner, and Elizabeth Heyrick's Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition, all to argue that calls for gradual emancipation were a slaveholder ploy that merely entrenches the evil.
questions.quote: Abolitionist movement leader William Lloyd Garrison said similarly, "Come what may – cost what it may – inscribe on the banner which you unfurl to the breeze, as your religious and political motto – 'NO COMPROMISE WITH SLAVERY!'"  There are innumerable principled and pragmatic reasons why Garrison was correct. May we similarly proclaim "NO COMPROMISE WITH CHILD SACRIFICE!"

questions.question: Why does Silberman call SB1721, the Unborn Person Equality Act, hollow despite its language about personhood?
questions.audience: Legislator or staffer evaluating incremental bills
questions.answer: He argues that Section 2 Part C makes every protection conditional on the Supreme Court agreeing, which negates the bill given that the federal courts have already rejected such positions 37 times.
questions.quote: In other words, this bill establishes that life begins at conception **if the supreme court agrees** that life begins at conception. It establishes that there is a right to life and equal protection **if the supreme court agrees** that there is a right to life and equal protection.

questions.question: What is the article's core complaint about SB1728, the Unborn Person Wrongful Death Act?
questions.audience: Legislator or staffer evaluating incremental bills
questions.answer: Silberman says the bill calls the unborn child a person yet still permits the killing, only requiring the abortionist to follow a procedural checklist to make the death 'rightful.' Senator Bullard reportedly admitted on the floor that the checklist is the bill's whole substance and even called it the best way to protect babies.
questions.quote: **Senate Bill 1728 fully affirms the legality of the practice of murdering unborn children. It simply provides the abortionist with a few hoops to jump through to make the unborn person's death rightful.**

questions.question: Why does Silberman dismiss HB1396, the sex-selection abortion ban, as pointless?
questions.answer: Sex of the baby usually isn't disclosed until 18-21 weeks, but abortion is already illegal in Oklahoma after 20 weeks, so the bill bans something that is largely already prohibited.
questions.quote: This bill makes it illegal to have an abortion if the mother says she is aware of the sex of the child. Parents are typically first offered information about the sex of the child when the baby is 18-21 weeks. Abortion is already illegal in Oklahoma after 20 weeks, meaning the bill has no point.

questions.question: How does Silberman read Heyrick's argument about why gradualism is satanic?
questions.audience: Historian comparing antislavery and antiabortion rhetoric
questions.answer: He quotes Heyrick's claim that 'gradual emancipation' originated with slaveholders themselves because contemplating gradual abolition breeds gradual indifference, allowing people to tolerate evil indefinitely. Silberman applies the same logic to incremental abortion bills.
questions.quote: "But this GRADUAL ABOLITION, has been the grand marplot of human virtue and happiness; the very masterpiece of satanic policy. By converting the cry for immediate, into gradual emancipation, the prince of slave holders, 'transformed himself, with astonishing dexterity, into an angel of light,' and thereby 'deceived the very elect.'"

questions.question: Why does Silberman think the establishment is panicking specifically about Hamilton's race?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist seeking talking points against compromise
questions.answer: With Joseph Silk leaving the Senate to run for Congress, the establishment doesn't want another abolitionist on the floor pressing for SB13-style bills, and Hamilton plus other endorsed candidates threaten to install several.
questions.quote: The establishment is flailing with absurd attack mailers like this one because Warren has a real chance to unseat Boggs and introduce a bill to abolish abortion in the State Senate.

questions.question: What is the article's bottom-line framing of the choice in Senate District 7?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter weighing the Boggs vs. Hamilton primary
questions.answer: Silberman reduces the contest to a single sentence: Boggs regulates abortion, Hamilton abolishes it.
questions.quote: Don't be fooled by the establishment's use of a dark money group to attempt to deceive you. Larry Boggs is running to regulate abortion. Warren Hamilton is running to abolish it.

related.title: Oklahomans for Life Asks Candidates to Oppose Abortion's Immediate Abolition
related.reason: Directly substantiates the article's claim about what an OFL 100% score actually requires; the post links to it explicitly.

related.title: Senate Bill 1728 Fully Affirms Legal Abortion
related.reason: Expands the SB1728 critique that Silberman summarizes as one of the three bills Hamilton would rightly oppose.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the broader framework for why incremental compromises are counterproductive, which is the central argument of the Hamilton piece.

related.title: James Lankford Should Revoke His Endorsement of Larry Boggs
related.reason: Companion campaign-cycle attack on Hamilton's opponent, naming the same dynamics around Boggs's endorsements.

related.title: Larry Boggs Campaigns With 2019 & 2020 Abortion Rights Men of the Year
related.reason: Follow-up published days later that continues the case against Boggs in the Hamilton race and ties him to Treat and McCortney.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Documents the SB13 denial and Treat's incremental face-saving that Silberman uses as Exhibit A in this article.

related.title: Proof David Bullard is Lying About SB13 to Pastors and Oklahomans
related.reason: Backs up the SB1728/Bullard floor admission referenced in the section dissecting the Wrongful Death Act.
